answer: Yes, the contract limits liability in several ways: it excludes indirect, incidental, consequential, special, and punitive damages (with certain exceptions), sets a general monetary limit of U.S.$5,000,000 for causes of action arising under the Agreement, increases the limit to U.S.$10,000,000 for breaches of Article II, and limits ETI's liability for intellectual property indemnity to 50% of monies paid by Nortel up to a maximum of U.S.$1,000,000. citations.text: In no event shall either Party be liable for any special or punitive damages arising from breach of this Agreement.

ETI's liability to Nortel arising from or relating to the intellectual     property indemnity set forth in the Enterprise License shall not exceed 50%     (fifty percent) of the monies paid by Nortel thereunder to a maximum of     U.S.$1,000,000 (one million U.S. dollars).

title: MIDDLEBROOKPHARMACEUTICALS,INC_03_18_2010-EX-10.1-PROMOTION AGREEMENT
answer: The provided passages do not mention a general cap or limit on a party's liability, although they do state that parties are excused from liability for failures or delays in performance caused by force majeure events, and that MBRK has no liability for the acts or omissions of DD or its Representatives.

citations.text:
Each party shall be excused from liability for the failure or delay in performance of any obligation under this Agreement  by reason of any extraordinary, unexpected and/or unavoidable event, such as acts of God, fire, flood, explosion,  earthquake, or other natural forces, war, civil unrest, accident, destruction or other casualty, any lack or failure of  transportation facilities, any lack or







failure of supply of raw materials, any strike or labor disturbance, or any other event similar to those enumerated above.
MBRK  has no duty, liability or responsibility of any kind, to or for the acts or omissions of DD or any of DD's Representatives.

title: ALLIANCEBANCORPINCOFPENNSYLVANIA_10_18_2006-EX-1.2-AGENCY AGREEMENT
answer: The contract limits liability in two ways: first, it stipulates that the Agent has no liability (direct, indirect, in contract, tort, or otherwise) to the MHCs and its members, the Bank, or the creditors of the Company, MHCs, or Bank regarding the Agent's engagement or performance; second, it limits the indemnifying parties' liability for fees and expenses to no more than one counsel (and no more than one local counsel per jurisdiction) separate from their own counsel for related actions in the same jurisdiction arising from the same general allegations or circumstances.

citations.text: the Agent shall not have any liability (whether direct or indirect, in contract or tort or otherwise) to the MHCs and its members, the Bank, the Company's, the MHCs's or the Bank's creditors relating to or arising out of the engagement of the Agent pursuant to, or the performance by the A

In no event shall the indemnifying parties be liable for fees and expenses of more than one counsel (in addition to no more than one local counsel in each separate jurisdiction in which any action or proceeding is commenced) separate from their own counsel for all indemnified parties in connection with any one action or separate but similar or related actions in the same jurisdiction arising out of the same general allegations or circumstances.
